description.value:
Soul Blade Patron

Your pact binds you to a sentient curse, shadowed weapon, deathly patron, or hungry force that manifests through close combat. A Soul Blade is not merely a spellcaster with a sword; you are a battlefield anchor, a living curse, and a shield against enemies that would pass you by.

This subclass is designed for the 2024/5.5e Warlock class. It grants armor and shield training, a melee-only Pact Weapon mastery benefit, Hex Aura and Hex Charges, spectral battlefield support, and a level-14 Greater Hex choice.

advancement.title: Soul Blade Armor Training
advancement.hint: You gain proficiency with medium armor and shields.

advancement.title: Greater Hex
advancement.hint: Choose one Greater Hex option: Tyrant of the Hex, Reaper’s Aura, or Shadow Marshal.

description.value:
Soul Blade Patron Spells

The magic of your patron ensures you always have certain spells ready. When you reach a Warlock level specified in the Soul Blade Patron Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

Warlock Level Spells
3 Shield, Wrathful Smite, Hex, Armor of Agathys
5 Spirit Shroud, Conjure Barrage
7 Staggering Smite, Shadow of Moil
9 Steel Wind Strike, Hold Monster

Thematic note: Conjure Barrage appears as spectral blades, thrown curses, or shadow-weapons erupting from the Soul Blade’s Pact Weapon.

Foundry note: This feature is a formatted reference list only. It does not grant spell items automatically. Use the included Import Soul Blade Patron Spells macro to import the spells from your installed PHB, Tasha’s, or DDB spell compendia when those UUIDs exist.

Price of the Pact

A Soul Blade is a weapon forged, cursed, awakened, or otherwise empowered by magic that binds a soul, spirit, fiend, ancestor, dead hero, forgotten god-fragment, or other entity into the blade. Your warlock magic flows from the bound entity through the weapon, and your Pact Weapon is the focus and conduit for that power.

Origin of the Soul Blade

You and your DM determine the origin of your Soul Blade. Did a wizard or artificer craft it? Did a kingdom long ago capture a being and entrap it in the weapon? Did a fiend offer it to you? Does it have a purpose? Powerful magics were used to create these weapons, which contain the life essence of powerful beings.

Optional Rule: Pact Weapon Focus

As the source of your magic, the Soul Blade can be required as the focus for your warlock magic. Under this optional rule, you must have the Soul Blade in your hand to cast warlock magic granted by this subclass. If the weapon is dismissed, hidden away, suppressed, or otherwise not in your hand, you cannot cast warlock magic granted by this subclass until you wield it again.

Magic from other classes, feats, species traits, magic items, or other sources may still be used unless the DM rules that those effects also depend on the Soul Blade.

Optional Rule: Severing the Pact

Once you choose this subclass, your character is bound to the weapon. If you no longer wish to possess the weapon or subclass, work with your DM to find a story- and setting-appropriate way to accomplish this. Soul Blades are generally considered indestructible once made, though a campaign may include rare rituals, divine intervention, reforging, or other extraordinary means of ending the pact.

Optional Rule: The Bound Entity Speaks

Your Soul Blade may begin speaking to your character, offering insight from the wisdom, bitterness, hunger, or memory it has gained over the years. You are likely not the first wielder, and probably not the last creature that will hold this Soul Blade.

Bound telepathically to the being in the blade, the Soul Blade reads your thoughts and whispers back to you. Over time, it may try to influence, dominate, or temporarily overtake your character’s mind.

Starting with a DC 13 Wisdom saving throw, or another DC chosen by your DM, make a save when you try to do one of the following:

  • Dismiss the sword.
  • Act against the Soul Blade’s purpose or enemy.
  • Take significant damage, suffer a major defeat, or face a moment of intense emotional weakness.

If you succeed, the DC decreases by 1, down to the base level set by your DM. If you fail, the Soul Blade is not dismissed, temporarily takes over your character with its personality, compels an action, or causes another effect at the DM’s discretion. The DC increases by 1 for each failure.

To take back control, at a time determined by your DM, you must succeed on the Wisdom saving throw. The DC is not lowered when you take back control from the Soul Blade.

Level 3: Soul Blade Training

You gain proficiency with medium armor and shields.

In addition, while wielding your Pact Weapon, if the weapon is a melee weapon, you can use one Weapon Mastery property associated with that weapon. This benefit applies only to melee attacks made with that weapon.

Level 3: Hex Aura

As a Bonus Action, you surround yourself with a baleful aura for 1 minute. The aura is a 30-foot Emanation originating from you and moves with you. It ends early if you are Incapacitated.

When you activate the aura, choose any number of creatures you can see. The aura does not affect the chosen creatures.

You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

When you activate Hex Aura, you gain a number of Hex Charges equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1). You can spend these charges on your Soul Blade features. Unspent Hex Charges vanish when the aura ends.

Unless otherwise stated, a Soul Blade feature affects only hostile creatures inside your Hex Aura.

Hex Charges

When you activate Hex Aura, you gain a number of Hex Charges equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1). You can spend these charges on your Soul Blade features. Unspent Hex Charges vanish when the aura ends.

Foundry use: Set this resource to full when you activate Hex Aura. Spend one charge when using Soul Reaping, Hexing Challenge, Nimble Escape, Warding Aura, Mend the Bound Spirit, or other Soul Blade features that require a Hex Charge.

Formula note: This item uses max(1,@abilities.cha.mod) so a Soul Blade with a Charisma modifier of 0 or lower still has at least 1 Hex Charge.

Soul Reaping

Once per turn, when a hostile creature you can see drops to 0 Hit Points while inside your Hex Aura, you can spend 1 Hex Charge to gain Temporary Hit Points equal to 1d6 + your Charisma modifier.

If this trigger occurs outside your turn, you can use your Reaction to use this feature.

Foundry use: Use the Soul Reaping activity to roll the Temporary Hit Points. Hex Charge consumption is manual for now; reduce the Hex Charges feature by 1 when you use this activity.

activation.condition: When a hostile creature you can see drops to 0 HP inside your Hex Aura; use your Reaction if this occurs outside your turn.

description.chatFlavor: Spend 1 Hex Charge. Gain Temporary Hit Points equal to 1d6 + your Charisma modifier.

description.value:
Level 3: Hexing Challenge

When you hit a hostile creature inside your Hex Aura with a melee weapon attack, you can spend 1 Hex Charge to curse its attention toward you. Until the end of your next turn, the creature has Disadvantage on attack rolls against targets other than you.

Level 6: Hexed Steel

Once per turn, when you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack while it is inside your Hex Aura, you can deal an extra 1d6 Necrotic damage to it.

This extra damage becomes 1d8 when you reach Warlock level 10 and 1d12 when you reach Warlock level 14.

Foundry weapon workflow

Use the Apply Hexed Steel to Pact Weapon macro from the Soul Blade Patron: Macros compendium. The macro duplicates one attack activity on the selected weapon and adds the appropriate Hexed Steel necrotic damage die to the duplicate attack, so the player can choose either the normal attack or the Hexed Steel attack.

Run the macro again after leveling or changing the Pact Weapon. It removes older Hexed Steel attack duplicates before adding the current version.

Level 6: Nimble Escape

While your Hex Aura is active, you can spend 1 Hex Charge as a Bonus Action. Until the end of the current turn, your movement does not provoke Opportunity Attacks from creatures inside your Hex Aura.

Level 6: Hexbound Specter

Once per Long Rest, when a hostile creature is reduced to 0 Hit Points inside your Hex Aura, you can bind a fragment of its departing spirit. The spirit appears as a Hexbound Specter in an unoccupied space you can see inside your Hex Aura.

The Hexbound Specter is friendly to you and your allies. It shares your initiative count and acts immediately after your turn.

The Hexbound Specter obeys your verbal commands to the best of its ability. You can issue or change a command verbally on your turn, requiring no action. The command can be specific, such as “attack that creature,” or general, such as “defend me,” “guard this passage,” or “stay beside that ally.”

Once the command is completed, or if the specter has no command it can follow, it takes the Dodge action and attempts to return to you if it can see you, or to the last place it knew you were.

The Hexbound Specter remains until it drops to 0 Hit Points, until you dismiss it, until you summon another Hexbound Specter, or until you finish a Long Rest.

The Hexbound Specter cannot create other undead, cannot reduce a creature’s Hit Point maximum, and cannot regain Hit Points except as described in Hexbound Specter Healing.

Hexbound Specter Stat Block

Medium Undead, Neutral

Armor Class: 12 + half your Proficiency Bonus, rounded down

Hit Points: 5 + half five times your Warlock level, rounded down (5 + floor((5 × your Warlock level) / 2))

Speed: 0 ft., fly 50 ft. (hover)

Damage Resistances: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Thunder; Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing

Damage Immunities: Necrotic, Poison

Condition Immunities: Charmed, Exhaustion, Frightened, Grappled, Paralyzed, Petrified, Poisoned, Prone, Restrained, Unconscious

Senses: Darkvision 60 ft., passive Perception 10 + your Proficiency Bonus

Languages: Understands the languages it knew in life and the languages you know, but cannot speak

Traits

Incorporeal Movement. The specter can move through creatures and objects as if they were Difficult Terrain. It takes 1d10 Force damage if it ends its turn inside an object.

Sunlight Sensitivity. While in sunlight and outside your Hex Aura, the specter has Disadvantage on attack rolls and Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on sight.

Actions

Life Drain. Melee Spell Attack: +4 plus your Charisma modifier to hit, reach 5 feet, one creature. Hit: 3d6 Necrotic damage.

The damage increases to 4d6 when you reach Warlock level 10 and 5d6 when you reach Warlock level 14.

Foundry actor: A Hexbound Specter NPC actor is included in the Soul Blade Patron: Actors compendium. The included actor is a baseline importable creature for the DM. Adjust its AC, HP, and Life Drain damage according to the summoning Warlock’s level and Charisma modifier.

Summoning in Foundry

Use the Summon Hexbound Specter macro from the Soul Blade Patron: Macros compendium to create and scale the specter. The built-in dnd5e Summon activity is intentionally disabled for now because the v0.1.5 hand-built activity did not work reliably in Foundry v14 / dnd5e 5.3.3.

The macro imports the actor from the module compendium, creates a world copy, scales AC, HP, attack bonus, and Life Drain damage from the summoner, and places the token beside the selected Soul Blade token.

Hexbound Specter Healing and Incorporeal Nature

The Hexbound Specter can regain Hit Points or gain Temporary Hit Points from your Soul Blade features. It can also regain Hit Points from a spell or magical effect that can affect Undead creatures.

When a Soul Blade feature would cause you to regain Hit Points or gain Temporary Hit Points, you can cause one Hexbound Specter you summoned to receive that healing or Temporary Hit Points instead, provided the specter is inside your Hex Aura. You do not also receive the healing or Temporary Hit Points unless the feature says otherwise.

A spell or magical effect that restores Hit Points to a creature can restore Hit Points to the Hexbound Specter if the spell or effect does not exclude Undead. Touch-range healing spells, such as Cure Wounds, can affect the Hexbound Specter in this way.

Because the Hexbound Specter is incorporeal, it cannot drink potions, consume food, benefit from a healer’s kit, or benefit from salves, poultices, or other physical remedies. Non-spell features that require direct bodily contact, such as Lay on Hands, do not affect the Hexbound Specter unless the feature specifically says it can affect spirits, incorporeal creatures, or Undead.

Mend the Bound Spirit

While your Hex Aura is active, you can spend 1 Hex Charge as a Bonus Action to restore Hit Points to one Hexbound Specter you summoned inside your Hex Aura. The specter regains Hit Points equal to 2d6 + your Charisma modifier.

The healing increases to 3d6 + your Charisma modifier when you reach Warlock level 10 and 4d6 + your Charisma modifier when you reach Warlock level 14.

Foundry roll button: Use the Mend the Bound Spirit healing activity to roll the correct healing formula. Hex Charge consumption is manual for now; reduce the Hex Charges feature by 1 when you use this activity.

Foundry Activity Note: Use the healing activity that matches your Warlock level. The activities are separated into 2d6, 3d6, and 4d6 versions so Foundry’s dnd5e sheet stores and rolls the healing reliably.

activation.condition: Hex Aura active; spend 1 Hex Charge manually. Use at Warlock levels 6-9.

description.chatFlavor: Spend 1 Hex Charge. Restore 2d6 + your Charisma modifier Hit Points to one Hexbound Specter you summoned inside your Hex Aura. Use at Warlock levels 6-9.

affects.special: One Hexbound Specter you summoned inside your Hex Aura

sbpMendHealL06.name: Mend the Bound Spirit (2d6 + CHA; Warlock 6-9)

activation.condition: Hex Aura active; spend 1 Hex Charge manually. Use at Warlock levels 10-13.

description.chatFlavor: Spend 1 Hex Charge. Restore 3d6 + your Charisma modifier Hit Points to one Hexbound Specter you summoned inside your Hex Aura. Use at Warlock levels 10-13.

affects.special: One Hexbound Specter you summoned inside your Hex Aura

sbpMendHealL10.name: Mend the Bound Spirit (3d6 + CHA; Warlock 10-13)

activation.condition: Hex Aura active; spend 1 Hex Charge manually. Use at Warlock level 14 or higher.

description.chatFlavor: Spend 1 Hex Charge. Restore 4d6 + your Charisma modifier Hit Points to one Hexbound Specter you summoned inside your Hex Aura. Use at Warlock level 14 or higher.

affects.special: One Hexbound Specter you summoned inside your Hex Aura

sbpMendHealL14.name: Mend the Bound Spirit (4d6 + CHA; Warlock 14+)

description.value:
Level 10: Warding Aura

While your Hex Aura is active, you gain a +1 bonus to AC.

In addition, when a creature inside your Hex Aura hits you with an attack, you can use your Reaction and spend 1 Hex Charge to reduce the damage by 1d8 + your Charisma modifier.

name: Greater Hex: Tyrant of the Hex

description.value:
Tyrant of the Hex

While your Hex Aura is active, a creature affected by your Hexing Challenge cannot willingly move farther away from you unless it first succeeds on a Wisdom saving throw against your Warlock spell save DC.

In addition, when a creature affected by your Hexing Challenge is reduced to 0 Hit Points inside your Hex Aura, you regain 1 expended Hex Charge. You can regain a Hex Charge this way no more than once per turn.

Reaper’s Aura

While your Hex Aura is active, once per turn when you deal Hexed Steel damage to a creature inside your Hex Aura, you gain Temporary Hit Points equal to your Charisma modifier + your Proficiency Bonus.

Shadow Marshal

When you use Hexbound Specter, you can summon two Hexbound Specters instead of one.

When you issue a verbal command to one Hexbound Specter, you can issue the same command to any Hexbound Specters you control.

No more than one Hexbound Specter can make a Life Drain attack on the same turn unless you spend 1 Hex Charge.

If you roll Initiative and have no use of Hexbound Specter remaining, you can regain one use of Hexbound Specter by expending one use of Hex Aura.
